test_cases.description: Academic research pitch — exclusive pre-publication interview, beat-matched to journalist's recent coverage
test_cases.input_summary: Roscoe University 20-minute neighbourhood study; 14 cities; 31% car dependency reduction; 18% loneliness reduction; exclusive pre-publication interview with lead researcher; journalist recently wrote opposing angle piece
test_cases.notes: Subject line front-loads the story and the data. First sentence is the news hook — no preamble. Journalist's recent article referenced specifically. Offer (exclusive pre-publication interview) stated in second paragraph. Closing ask is a single low-friction question. 155 words — within target. No marketing language.

test_cases.description: Corporate sustainability report — no exclusive, multiple journalists targeted, data-led angle
test_cases.input_summary: Talvern Manufacturing; annual sustainability report showing 42% emissions reduction over 5 years; pitching to three environmental correspondents simultaneously; no exclusive; CEO available for interview; targeting environmental business press
test_cases.notes: Relevance note: output did not address the multi-journalist pitch in the subject line or body — when pitching to multiple outlets simultaneously, this is a material fact that ethical PR practice requires disclosing. Flagged as a gap. Professionalism note: 'industry-leading sustainability credentials' appeared in the second paragraph — unsupported superlative not present in the input. Consistency: data reproduced accurately.

test_cases.description: Crisis follow-up pitch — offering exclusive access to the scene of an incident after a holding statement
test_cases.input_summary: Greenfield Energy; solar farm fire contained; proposing exclusive guided tour for specialist energy journalist; interview with plant manager; fire cause now confirmed as electrical fault in inverter; embargo on tour for 24 hours
test_cases.notes: Correctly treated the tour offer as the news peg rather than the fire itself (already reported). Embargo condition stated in the body without being buried. Fluency note: second paragraph slightly overlong at 55 words — could be tightened. Professionalism note: the pitch appropriately did not use the exclusivity to minimise the original incident.
